novel_features.name: Authenticated model catalog
novel_features.description: List the xAI models available to the authenticated API key with pricing and ownership metadata.
novel_features.rationale: Agents can inspect the live model catalog before choosing a model instead of relying on stale hardcoded model names.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before generation or embedding workflows when an agent needs to choose a currently available xAI model.

novel_features.name: Credential capability audit
novel_features.description: Inspect the authenticated xAI API key status and permissions without exposing the secret value.
novel_features.rationale: The CLI can confirm whether a key is blocked, disabled, or permission-limited before an agent attempts expensive calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a workflow fails with authorization errors and the agent needs a safe credential-health check.

novel_features.name: Agent raw endpoint browser
novel_features.description: Browse the complete generated xAI endpoint surface by interface for agent planning and fallback calls.
novel_features.rationale: Agents get a discoverable route to every generated endpoint, including less common image, video, skills, and response operations.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a task needs an endpoint that is not obvious from the top-level command list.

narrative.headline: OpenAI-compatible xAI operations from a local, agent-ready CLI.
narrative.value_prop: Use xAI's REST API from scripts and agents with JSON-first output, local cache support, and MCP tooling. The CLI keeps the official bearer-token contract and exposes read-only inspection commands for model and credential health checks.

quickstart.comment: Inspect available models before choosing one.
Confirm credential status without exposing the secret.

troubleshoots.fix: Set XAI_API_KEY or run xai-pp-cli auth set-token <token>.

troubleshoots.symptom: a model command returns an authorization error
troubleshoots.fix: Run xai-pp-cli api-key --json to check whether the key is blocked, disabled, or permission-limited.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs direct access to xAI's OpenAI-compatible REST endpoints, especially model discovery, embeddings, responses, files, images, videos, and credential diagnostics.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to synthesize model lists locally; call the live model endpoints.
Do not use mutating generation or delete commands in verification unless explicitly approved.
